title: refloraR
text:
refloraR is an R package for accessing and analyzing plant specimen data from the REFLORA Virtual Herbarium, hosted by the Rio de Janeiro Botanical Garden. It provides tools for downloading, summarizing, and filtering herbarium records in Darwin Core Archive (DwC-A) format via the REFLORA IPT.


You can install the development version of refloraR from GitHub with:
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("DBOSlab/refloraR")
library(refloraR)



A general description of the available main functions (reflora_download and reflora_summary) that extract original REFLORA collections are provided below.


The following code can be used to extract a summary of all REFLORA-associated collections, including herbarium acronym, curator’s email contact, number of records and a direct link to the original REFLORA Integrated Publishing Toolkit (IPT).
library(refloraR)

summary_df <- reflora_summary(verbose = TRUE,
                              save = TRUE,
                              dir = "reflora_summary")
By specifying a vector of herbarium acronyms, the user can extract a summary for just the specific herbarium collection.
summary_some_df <- reflora_summary(herbarium = c("ALCB", "RB", "HUEFS", "US", "K"),
                                   verbose = TRUE,
                                   save = TRUE,
                                   dir = "reflora_summary")



The following code can be used to download original specimen records in DwC-A format and associated metada for all REFLORA collections.
library(refloraR)

reflora_download(verbose = TRUE,
                 dir = "reflora_download")
By specifying a vector of herbarium acronyms, the user can download specimens records for just the specific herbarium collection.
reflora_download(herbarium = c("ALCB", "HUEFS", "RB", "US", "K"),
                 verbose = TRUE,
                 dir = "reflora_download")




Full function documentation and articles are available at the refloraR website.



Cardoso, D. & Calderón, C. 2025. refloraR: An R Package for Exploring Plant Specimen Collections from REFLORA Virtual Herbarium. https://github.com/dboslab/refloraR

title: RedeC2
text:
Research Network · Brazil
      RedeC2
      Permanent plots for understanding Caatinga and Cerrado dynamics across Brazil's seasonally dry ecosystems.
      CaatingaCerradoLong-term plotsDry ecosystems
      Explore sitesSee protocols
      2target biomes∞long-term monitoring+10partner institutionsBRnational network
    
    A collaborative platform for comparable field data, ecological synthesis and evidence-based conservation in Caatinga and Cerrado systems.
  


  The RedeC2 network
  
    RedeC2 is a collaborative research network dedicated to understanding the multifunctional dynamics of Brazil's seasonally dry tropical ecosystems, especially Caatinga and Cerrado.Bringing together researchers from multiple institutions, the network integrates permanent plots, floristic inventories, functional traits and environmental data to investigate how plant communities are structured and how they respond to climatic seasonality, soil conditions and landscape context.Across different sites and regions, RedeC2 studies how natural gradients and human-driven disturbances influence vegetation structure, successional pathways and the spatial turnover of species.
    Overarching goalGenerate robust, comparable data that support conservation planning, ecological restoration and evidence-based management of dry ecosystems in Brazil.FloristicsDynamicsRestorationOpen workflows
  


  Sites, data, and outputs
  SitesPermanent plots across Caatinga and Cerrado formations.GradientsSeasonality, soils, structure and landscape context.ProductsComparable data for conservation, restoration and synthesis.

Integrated monitoring workflow01 · Permanent plotsRepeated measurements of woody vegetation, herbs, regeneration and coarse woody debris in standardized units.02 · Species and traitsFloristic inventories, taxonomic standardization and functional trait records connected to ecological gradients.03 · Environmental contextClimate, soil and landscape layers combined with field records for cross-site comparability.04 · SynthesisOutputs for ecological theory, biodiversity conservation, restoration planning and public decision-making.





Participating institutions




Empresa Brasileira de Pesquisa Agropecuária (EMBRAPA)



Institut de Recherche pour le Développement (IRD)



Instituto de Pesquisas Jardim Botânico do Rio de Janeiro (JBRJ)



Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew



Serviço Florestal Brasileiro (SFB)



Universidade do Estado de Mato Grosso (UNEMAT – Cáceres)



Universidade Estadual Paulista (UNESP – Rio Claro)



Universidade Federal de Lavras (UFLA)



Universidade Federal de Minas Gerais (UFMG)



Universidade Federal de Pernambuco (UFPE)



Universidade Federal do Ceará (UFC)



Universidade Federal do Vale do São Francisco (UNIVASF)



Università di Torino (UniTO)



University of Exeter





ContactEmailc2redec2@gmail.comRepositorygithub.com/C2rede

title: Protocols
text:
Field methods · Data standards · Partner manualsProtocolsfor C2Standardized protocols are the backbone of long-term monitoring: they keep data comparable across sites, teams and years.CaatingaCerradoPermanent plots
Protocol logicRede C2 works with permanent plots in dry ecosystems and connects with broader monitoring initiatives.This page gathers core protocol references that can support plot establishment, remeasurement, field standardization, data harmonization and cross-network comparability.Recommended anchorThe DRYFLOR field manual is especially relevant because it offers a standardized workflow for permanent plots in seasonally dry tropical forests, including plot layout, tagging, remeasurement and woody stem monitoring.DRYFLOR DOIPlotsLayout, tagging and remeasurement.Woody stemsDiameter, recruits and mortality.HerbsRegeneration and herb layer modules.ModulesSoils, traits and disturbance.
Core protocol referencesRede C2Network baseSpace for Rede C2’s own protocols, field sheets and coding standards for plot installation, recensus, disturbance records, traits and soils.Plot protocolDatasheetsField codesDRYFLOR field manualDry forest permanent plotsA key manual for establishing and remeasuring permanent plots in seasonally dry tropical forests. Useful for woody stems, tagging rules and long-term comparability.Protocols pagePortuguêsEnglishForestPlots.netField resourcesField resources, publications and practical material that support tropical plot monitoring and interoperability across networks.No campoPublicationsSECODry tropics compatibilityProtocol pages and modules aligned with DryFlor, ForestPlots and related networks for dry-tropics monitoring.ProtocolsProjectSEOSAWLinked woodland modulesInteroperable protocols and datasheets for woodland monitoring, useful as a reference for compatible modules and workflow design.ManualsCitation anchorPortuguese DRYFLOR manualUse the DOI as a stable citation anchor when referring to the Portuguese DRYFLOR field manual in Rede C2 methods and protocol references.DOI
Suggested Rede C2 workflow01Plot establishment and remeasurementPlot geometry, subplot layout, coordinates, tagging, recensus intervals and replacement rules.02Woody stems censusDiameter standards, multiple stems, damage codes, recruits, mortality and field note structure.03Herb layer and regenerationModules for herbs, seedlings and regeneration tracking compatible with dry-forest and savanna workflows.04Soils, traits and disturbanceOptional compatible modules inspired by partner networks to maximize interoperability and ecological interpretation.
Datasheets and standardsDatasheets and field sheetsMinimum tables for recording plot metadata, stems, recruits, mortality, disturbance notes and observer information.Plot metadataStemsObserversField codes and data standardsControlled vocabularies for species status, stem condition, damage, phenology, collection status and data-quality flags.Controlled vocabularyQuality flagsHarmonization

title: Sites - Rede C2
text:
Permanent plots · Caatinga + CerradoSites& gradientsExplore the spatial structure of Rede C2 field sites and the environmental gradients that connect dry forests, savannas, soils and seasonality.CaatingaCerradoInteractive map

Interactive mapUse the map to inspect Rede C2 permanent plots, biome classification, plot area, census number and climatic or institutional metadata available in the project table.






Tip: keep the image carousel light by using optimized WebP/JPG versions and only load high-resolution images when necessary.
How to read the networkSpatial coverageSites are distributed across formations and regions that capture ecological turnover in dry ecosystems.Environmental gradientsClimate, soil, vegetation structure and landscape context should be interpreted jointly with plot-level data.Long-term comparabilityRepeated censuses make it possible to connect local dynamics with biome-wide patterns.
